title: Cache-control directive semantics
description: s-maxage governs shared (CDN) caches, max-age governs browsers, and private/no-store control whether the edge may cache at all.
body: Use when setting caching headers. max-age applies to private (browser) caches; s-maxage overrides it for shared CDN caches; private forbids shared caching; no-store forbids all caching; stale-while-revalidate/stale-if-error extend serving past expiry. Do not conflate max-age with s-maxage — a CDN honors s-maxage for shared TTL. Actions: set s-maxage for edge TTL, private/no-store for per-user data, and stale directives for resilience. Validation signals include the edge caching public content and never caching private. Risk boundary: omitting private on authenticated responses risks public caching of user data.
